name: SOL Retest Continuation Runner v1
thesis: SOL 4h breakouts in transition often work better after a delayed retest than on the initial break. The edge should come from breakout continuation only once the retest holds, with partial profit and a runner for persistence.
edge_mechanism: delayed breakout continuation after successful retest
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest logic still collapses into sparse no-trade grammar, contradictory confirmation overlap, or fails to produce durable post-cost edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Breakout continuation only after price retests the broken area and holds instead of failing immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed entry after retest can filter false first-break moves while still leaving enough persistence for a runner.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h often trends in impulsive bursts followed by short resets, making retest logic structurally plausible.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If breakouts do not retest cleanly or persistence is too weak, entries may be underrepresented.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If there is some participation but low density, widen retest proximity before altering runner management.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest and persistence filters point in the same direction
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad retest proximity and modest ADX floor
